introduction: The documentary reveals the identity of the Carajas Indians Indian Reservation of Bananal Island (TO). Shot in Bauru (SP), the Pantanal (MS and MT) and the Isle of Bananal (TO), the film uses the Midwest region of Brazil to be important events in the history of the country: Monsoon, Roncador-Xingu Expedition (Brothers Villas Boas) and deactivation of the Train of Pantanal. Set biomes wetland and savannah, the photograph of the film is a spectacle. The soundtrack also stands out because it was totally sold by regional artists of the Pantanal and its flagship music Baila Me, Rita Lee and Roberto de Carvalho.
